events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: commercial construction, fire alarm devices & circuits, observed missing end-of-line resistor, work tested/inspected. Reference NFPA 72. Missing EOL resistor is non-compliant; fire alarm initiating device circuits require EOL resistor to supervise wiring, detect opens. Decision fail. Risk high? Missing EOL means circuit not supervised; open faults not detected, impairing fire alarm operation. Could be high or critical? In a tested/inspected stage, missing EOL is a code violation and functional impairment. Risk high. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 90? Value not asked, leave 0. Sound_source etc default empty? Schema says string for sound_source, event_time number, rate number, order array. Use defaults: sound_source "" event_time 0 rate 0 order empty. Workable empty. Findings: missing end-of-line resistor, initiating circuit not supervised. Actions: install correct EOL resistor per manufacturer/NFPA 72, retest circuit, verify supervision. Rationale. References NFPA 72 (e.g., 2019 17.6.3? Actually NFPA 72 requires EOL resistors for initiating device circuits; Class B requires EOL device to monitor line. Also NFPA 72 14.4 testing). Need exact JSON only. Ensure no markdown.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":":fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"tested/inspected","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":90,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["End-of-line resistor missing from fire alarm initiating device circuit","Circuit supervision is impaired; open faults will not be detected","Device/circuit is non-compliant with NFPA 72 supervision requirements"],"actions":["Install the correct value end-of-line resistor at the last device on each affected circuit per manufacturer instructions and NFPA 72","Verify circuit wiring configuration (Class B/Style) and supervision at the control panel","Retest the circuit and confirm normal supervisory condition and alarm operation","Document correction and obtain re-inspection"],"rationale":"NFPA 72 requires fire alarm initiating device circuits to be supervised so that opens or faults are annunciated. A missing end-of-line resistor defeats that supervision, leaving the circuit non-functional for fault detection and the installation non-compliant. Because the work has already been tested/inspected, this is a failed inspection item requiring correction.","references":["NFPA 72 - Fire Alarm Systems - initiating device circuit supervision requirements","NFPA 72 - end-of-line resistor requirements for Class B/Style B circuits"]}
